hinge: Amalgams appearing elevated with flat shiny surfaces years later indicate acid erosion of surrounding enamel (GERD, dietary acids), not faulty restorations. Investigate etiology before any restorative replacement or vertical dimension changes.
The Marquis probe has alternating color-coded bands at 3 mm increments for periodontal probing. Other options are unrelated instruments or diagnoses.
Midface hypoplasia with exophthalmos (shallow orbits) and craniofacial deformity is classic for Apert syndrome, a craniosynostosis syndrome.
Odontogenic cavernous sinus thrombosis spreads from maxillary anterior infections via the infraorbital space and angular vein into the ophthalmic veins and cavernous sinus.
Maxillary sinusitis commonly refers pain to posterior maxillary teeth, mimicking odontogenic pain. Among listed options it is the most frequent non-odontogenic cause of dental pain.
A chronic midpalatal ulcer that perforates into the nasal cavity is classic for tertiary syphilitic gumma. TB can cause oral ulcers but syphilis is the classic midline perforating lesion.

Dark intrinsic stains require a bonded ceramic with sufficient opacity to mask discoloration. Etched porcelain veneers provide controllable thickness and superior esthetic masking compared to more translucent composite or bulkier zirconia.
Contaminated instruments held before sterilization must be kept moist in a holding/disinfectant solution to prevent debris from drying. Dry storage or plain water is inadequate for infection control.

After non-surgical periodontal therapy with residual deep pockets and horizontal bone loss, surgical pocket reduction aims to improve cleanability and arrest disease. Increasing compliance alone does not address the anatomical defect.

When multiple reasonable treatment options exist (save tooth vs extract/implant), after full informed consent on risks, prognosis, and costs, the final decision is driven by patient preferences and values.

Radiopaque lines on buccal cortical bone adjacent to a chronically infected tooth represent proliferative periostitis (Garre's osteomyelitis) — an onion-skin periosteal reaction, not primary osteosarcoma.
Multiple myeloma is a malignant clonal proliferation of plasma cells in bone marrow. Lymphocytes and macrophages are not the defining neoplastic cell population.
Smiling, closing eyes tightly, and wrinkling the forehead test muscles of facial expression innervated by cranial nerve VII (facial), not trigeminal sensory/motor function.

With an incomplete stem mentioning sore throat and low-grade fever, the listed herpesviruses include VZV as the keyed board answer for vesicular/systemic viral presentation. HSV and EBV remain differentials without more clinical detail.
Epinephrine-containing local anesthetic in an uncontrolled hyperthyroid patient can precipitate thyrotoxic crisis, presenting with agitation, tachycardia, and dizziness.
Unilateral painful, itchy facial rash with blisters in a dermatomal distribution is herpes zoster (VZV reactivation). Primary HSV typically presents as oral-labial clusters, not a dermatomal pattern.

Apexification of an immature nonvital tooth requires a material that induces an apical hard-tissue barrier. Among the listed options, tricalcium phosphate-based ceramic material is the only plausible apexification agent (modern preference is MTA/biodentine).
Zinc oxide-eugenol cements exhibit relatively high short-term solubility and disintegration in the oral environment compared to resin or glass-ionomer materials.
Multiple small yellowish submucosal spots on the lip mucosa are Fordyce granules — ectopic sebaceous glands. They are benign and not sialadenitis or malignancy.

Among salivary gland malignancies listed, mucoepidermoid carcinoma is the most common malignant salivary gland tumor overall (including minor glands of the lip). Note: the most common lip malignancy overall is SCC, but the options are limited to salivary tumors.
NSAIDs can trigger bronchospasm in patients with severe asthma via the aspirin/NSAID sensitivity pathway. Paracetamol (acetaminophen) is the preferred safe analgesic in this setting.

For an anxious asthmatic patient with benzodiazepine allergy, nitrous oxide-oxygen inhalation provides titratable anxiolysis without relying on benzodiazepines or general anesthesia, and is safe in controlled asthma.
Internal inflammatory root resorption requires vital pulp tissue at the resorptive front. An intact predentin/odontoblast layer protects mineralized dentin from clastic attack.

A full-coverage stabilization splint for bruxism should have even contacts on both anterior and posterior teeth to share occlusal load, keep the appliance stable, and avoid overloading any single segment.

Nicotinic stomatitis (white-gray palate with red puncta in a smoker) is a benign reactive keratotic change. Reassure the patient, counsel smoking cessation, and monitor — biopsy only if atypical or nonresolving.

Preserving the few remaining strategic teeth (#17,33,43,44) as overdenture abutments improves retention, support, proprioception, and ridge preservation compared to extracting all remaining teeth for conventional complete dentures.

Horizontal tooth mobility of approximately 1 mm faciolingually corresponds to Miller Grade II. However, the options are poorly formed; the keyed choice appears to identify this as a non-Grade I classification from discernible mobility.
Proximal surfaces inaccessible to rubber cups or bristle wheels are polished using dental tape or floss charged with fine prophy paste to safely clean and polish interproximal contacts.
Standard excisional biopsy uses an elliptical (fusiform) incision with length approximately 3× width so the wound can be closed primarily with minimal tension and no dog-ears.
With a truncated stem regarding biopsy site, the lip is the classic accessible site for minor salivary gland biopsy when that is the only coherent anatomic option among the listed choices.
